title: हनुमान चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
श्रीगुरु चरन सरोज रज, निज मनु मुकुरु सुधारि।
बरनउँ रघुबर बिमल जसु, जो दायकु फल चारि॥
बुद्धिहीन तनु जानिके, सुमिरौं पवन-कुमार।
बल बुद्धि बिद्या देहु मोहिं, हरहु कलेस बिकार॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय हनुमान ज्ञान गुन सागर।
जय कपीस तिहुँ लोक उजागर॥
राम दूत अतुलित बल धामा।
अंजनि-पुत्र पवनसुत नामा॥
महाबीर बिक्रम बजरंगी।
कुमति निवार सुमति के संगी॥
कंचन बरन बिराज सुबेसा।
कानन कुंडल कुंचित केसा॥
हाथ बज्र औ ध्वजा बिराजै।
काँधे मूँज जनेऊ साजै॥
शंकर सुवन केसरीनंदन।
तेज प्रताप महा जग बंदन॥
बिद्यावान गुनी अति चातुर।
राम काज करिबे को आतुर॥
प्रभु चरित्र सुनिबे को रसिया।
राम लखन सीता मन बसिया॥
सूक्ष्म रूप धरि सियहिं दिखावा।
बिकट रूप धरि लंक जरावा॥
भीम रूप धरि असुर सँहारे।
रामचंद्र के काज सँवारे॥
लाय सजीवन लखन जियाये।
श्रीरघुबीर हरषि उर लाये॥
रघुपति कीन्ही बहुत बड़ाई।
तुम मम प्रिय भरतहि सम भाई॥
सहस बदन तुम्हरो जस गावैं।
अस कहि श्रीपति कंठ लगावैं॥
सनकादिक ब्रह्मादि मुनीसा।
नारद सारद सहित अहीसा॥
जम कुबेर दिगपाल जहाँ ते।
कबि कोबिद कहि सके कहाँ ते॥
तुम उपकार सुग्रीवहिं कीन्हा।
राम मिलाय राज पद दीन्हा॥
तुम्हरो मंत्र बिभीषन माना।
लंकेश्वर भए सब जग जाना॥
जुग सहस्र जोजन पर भानू।
लील्यो ताहि मधुर फल जानू॥
प्रभु मुद्रिका मेलि मुख माहीं।
जलधि लाँघि गये अचरज नाहीं॥
दुर्गम काज जगत के जेते।
सुगम अनुग्रह तुम्हरे तेते॥
राम दुआरे तुम रखवारे।
होत न आज्ञा बिनु पैसारे॥
सब सुख लहै तुम्हारी सरना।
तुम रक्षक काहू को डर ना॥
आपन तेज सम्हारो आपै।
तीनों लोक हाँक तें काँपै॥
भूत पिसाच निकट नहिं आवै।
महाबीर जब नाम सुनावै॥
नासै रोग हरै सब पीरा।
जपत निरंतर हनुमत बीरा॥
संकट तें हनुमान छुड़ावै।
मन क्रम बचन ध्यान जो लावै॥
सब पर राम तपस्वी राजा।
तिन के काज सकल तुम साजा॥
और मनोरथ जो कोई लावै।
सोई अमित जीवन फल पावै॥
चारों जुग परताप तुम्हारा।
है परसिद्ध जगत उजियारा॥
साधु-संत के तुम रखवारे।
असुर निकंदन राम दुलारे॥
अष्ट सिद्धि नौ निधि के दाता।
अस बर दीन जानकी माता॥
राम रसायन तुम्हरे पासा।
सदा रहो रघुपति के दासा॥
तुम्हरे भजन राम को पावै।
जनम-जनम के दुख बिसरावै॥
अन्तकाल रघुबर पुर जाई।
जहाँ जन्म हरि-भक्त कहाई॥
और देवता चित्त न धरई।
हनुमत सेइ सर्ब सुख करई॥
संकट कटै मिटै सब पीरा।
जो सुमिरै हनुमत बलबीरा॥
जय जय जय हनुमान गोसाईं।
कृपा करहु गुरुदेव की नाईं॥
जो सत बार पाठ कर कोई।
छूटहि बंदि महा सुख होई॥
जो यह पढ़ै हनुमान चालीसा।
होय सिद्धि साखी गौरीसा॥
तुलसीदास सदा हरि चेरा।
कीजै नाथ हृदय महँ डेरा॥

॥ दोहा ॥
पवनतनय संकट हरन, मंगल मूरति रूप।
राम लखन सीता सहित, हृदय बसहु सुर भूप॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Shri Guru Charan Saroj Raj, Nij Manu Mukuru Sudhari.
Baranau Raghubar Bimal Jasu, Jo Dayaku Phal Chari.
Buddhiheen Tanu Jaanike, Sumirau Pavan-Kumar.
Bal Buddhi Vidya Dehu Mohi, Harahu Kalesh Bikaar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Hanuman Gyan Gun Sagar.
Jai Kapis Tihun Lok Ujagar.
Ram Doot Atulit Bal Dhama.
Anjani-Putra Pavansut Nama.
Mahabir Bikram Bajrangi.
Kumati Nivar Sumati Ke Sangi.
Kanchan Baran Biraj Subesa.
Kanan Kundal Kunchit Kesa.
Hath Bajra Au Dhvaja Birajai.
Kandhe Moonj Janeu Sajai.
Shankar Suvan Kesarinandan.
Tej Pratap Maha Jag Bandan.
Vidyavan Guni Ati Chatur.
Ram Kaj Karibe Ko Aatur.
Prabhu Charitra Sunibe Ko Rasiya.
Ram Lakhan Sita Man Basiya.
Sukshma Roop Dhari Siyahi Dikhava.
Vikat Roop Dhari Lanka Jarava.
Bhim Roop Dhari Asur Sanhare.
Ramchandra Ke Kaj Sanvare.
Lay Sajivan Lakhan Jiyaye.
Shri Raghubir Harashi Ur Laye.
Raghupati Kinhi Bahut Badai.
Tum Mam Priya Bharatahi Sam Bhai.
Sahas Badan Tumharo Jas Gavein.
As Kahi Shripati Kanth Lagavein.
Sanakadik Brahmadi Munisa.
Narad Sarad Sahit Ahisa.
Jam Kuber Digpal Jahan Te.
Kabi Kobid Kahi Sake Kahan Te.
Tum Upkar Sugrivahi Kinha.
Ram Milay Raj Pad Dinha.
Tumharo Mantra Vibhishan Mana.
Lankeshwar Bhaye Sab Jag Jana.
Jug Sahastra Jojan Par Bhanu.
Lilyo Tahi Madhur Phal Janu.
Prabhu Mudrika Meli Mukh Mahi.
Jaladhi Langhi Gaye Achraj Nahi.
Durgam Kaj Jagat Ke Jete.
Sugam Anugrah Tumhare Tete.
Ram Duare Tum Rakhvare.
Hot Na Aagya Binu Paisare.
Sab Sukh Lahai Tumhari Sarna.
Tum Rakshak Kahu Ko Dar Na.
Apan Tej Samharo Apai.
Teenon Lok Hank Te Kanpai.
Bhoot Pisach Nikat Nahi Avai.
Mahabir Jab Naam Sunavai.
Nase Rog Harai Sab Peera.
Japat Nirantar Hanumat Beera.
Sankat Te Hanuman Chhudavai.
Man Kram Bachan Dhyan Jo Lavai.
Sab Par Ram Tapasvi Raja.
Tin Ke Kaj Sakal Tum Saja.
Aur Manorath Jo Koi Lavai.
Soi Amit Jivan Phal Pavai.
Charon Jug Partap Tumhara.
Hai Parsiddh Jagat Ujiyara.
Sadhu-Sant Ke Tum Rakhvare.
Asur Nikandan Ram Dulare.
Ashta Siddhi Nau Nidhi Ke Data.
As Bar Din Janaki Mata.
Ram Rasayan Tumhare Pasa.
Sada Raho Raghupati Ke Dasa.
Tumhare Bhajan Ram Ko Pavai.
Janam-Janam Ke Dukh Bisravai.
Antkaal Raghubar Pur Jai.
Jahan Janm Hari-Bhakt Kahai.
Aur Devta Chitt Na Dharai.
Hanumat Sei Sarb Sukh Karai.
Sankat Katai Mitai Sab Peera.
Jo Sumirai Hanumat Balbeera.
Jai Jai Jai Hanuman Gosain.
Kripa Karahu Gurudev Ki Nain.
Jo Sat Bar Path Kar Koi.
Chhutahi Bandi Maha Sukh Hoi.
Jo Yah Padhai Hanuman Chalisa.
Hoy Siddhi Sakhi Gaurisa.
Tulsidas Sada Hari Chera.
Kijai Nath Hriday Mah Dera.

|| Doha ||
Pavantanay Sankat Haran, Mangal Murati Roop.
Ram Lakhan Sita Sahit, Hriday Basahu Sur Bhoop.
hindiMeaning:
श्री गुरु के चरण कमलों की धूल से अपने मन रूपी दर्पण को पवित्र करके, मैं श्री रघुवीर के निर्मल यश का वर्णन करता हूँ, जो चारों फल - धर्म, अर्थ, काम और मोक्ष को देने वाला है।

हे पवनकुमार! मैं आपको स्मरण करता हूँ। मुझे बल, बुद्धि और विद्या दीजिए और मेरे क्लेशों और विकारों को दूर कीजिए।

हे हनुमान जी! आपकी जय हो। आप ज्ञान और गुणों के सागर हैं। आपकी जय हो, कपीश्वर! आप तीनों लोकों में प्रसिद्ध हैं।
benefits: Classical tradition invokes this hymn for removal of fear (bhaya-nashana) and obstacles (vighna-hara)
Invokes the deity's qualities of strength, courage, and discernment
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of earnest wishes
Invokes the blessings of Lord Rama through Hanuman's mediation
Invoked in classical tradition for protection from ailments and affliction
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Hanuman Chalisa — हनुमान चालीसा — is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn offered to Lord Hanuman. This revered text originates from Tulsidas - Ramcharitmanas. Devotees recite this prayer to fulfill all wishes when recited with devotion and remove all fears and obstacles from life. It is also said to cure diseases and remove all suffering. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti to invoke divine grace. Comprising 43 verses, it can be completed in approximately 15 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Hanuman Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Hanuman Chalisa in Hindi & Sanskrit with transliteration, meaning & free PDF. The most powerful prayer to Lord Hanuman.
source: Goswami Tulsidas · standalone composition in Awadhi, late 16th century

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Hanuman Chalisa and in which language?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Chalisa was composed by Goswami Tulsidas (1532-1623 CE), the saint-poet of the Mughal era who also authored the Ramcharitmanas. It is written in the Awadhi language, a vernacular of the Hindi belt that was widely understood in 16th-century North India. According to traditional accounts, Tulsidas composed the hymn during his imprisonment at Fatehpur Sikri, and each of the forty chaupais is said to correspond to a day of his confinement. Awadhi was chosen specifically so that ordinary devotees, not just Sanskrit scholars, could memorise and recite the verses. The work has since become the most widely recited Hindu prayer, with an estimated 50 million daily reciters worldwide. (Sources: Wikipedia; Art of Living)

seoFaqs.question: How many verses are in the Hanuman Chalisa and what is its structure?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Chalisa contains 43 verses in total: two introductory dohas (couplets), forty chaupais (quatrains), and one concluding doha. The word 'chalisa' itself derives from 'chalis,' the Hindi word for forty, referring to the central forty chaupais. The opening doha invokes the guru's grace, the middle chaupais praise Hanuman's strength, wisdom, devotion to Rama, and his exploits in the Ramayana such as carrying the Sanjivani mountain and burning Lanka. The closing doha asks Hanuman to reside in the devotee's heart along with Rama, Lakshmana, and Sita. This compact 43-verse architecture is one reason the Chalisa is so easy to memorise. (Source: Wikipedia)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Hanuman Chalisa traditionally recited 108 times?
seoFaqs.answer: The number 108 is sacred across Hindu traditions: a japa mala (prayer rosary) contains 108 beads, and many mantras are prescribed in multiples of 108. Reciting the Hanuman Chalisa 108 times, called a 'Hanuman Chalisa Path,' is believed to align the devotee with cosmic vibration and is considered one of the highest forms of Hanuman bhakti. Practitioners undertake it during Hanuman Jayanti, on consecutive Tuesdays or Saturdays, or as a sankalpa for removing severe obstacles. A complete 108-recitation cycle takes approximately 2.5 to 3 hours and is often performed in groups (akhand path) at Hanuman temples. Devotees traditionally undertake it for protection from negativity, fulfilment of vows, and mental fortitude. (Sources: Sanatan Culture; Hanuman Temple Bangalore)

seoFaqs.question: Why are Tuesday and Saturday considered the best days to recite Hanuman Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: In the Hindu week, Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) are both dedicated to Hanuman. Tuesday is associated with Mars (Mangal) and embodies the qualities of strength, courage, and decisive action, all of which Hanuman personifies. Saturday is governed by Shani (Saturn), and tradition holds that Hanuman protects devotees from the harsh effects of Shani Dosha — a story rooted in Hanuman freeing Shani from Ravana's captivity, after which Shani vowed to spare Hanuman's bhaktas. Reciting the Chalisa on these two days is believed to be especially potent for overcoming fear, neutralising malefic planetary influences, and gaining sankat-mochan grace. Many devotees observe a fast on Tuesday or Saturday alongside the recitation. (Source: Hello My Yoga; Webdunia)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the closing doha 'Pavan Tanay Sankat Harana'?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing doha — 'Pavan tanay sankat harana, mangala murati roop / Ram Lakhan Sita sahit, hriday basahu sur bhoop' — translates as: 'O son of the wind-god, remover of all troubles, embodiment of auspiciousness, please reside in my heart along with Rama, Lakshmana, and Sita, O king of devas.' It is the heart of Tulsidas's prayer: rather than asking for material gifts, the poet asks Hanuman to settle in his heart together with the divine family of Ayodhya. The line reflects the central theological move of the Chalisa — Hanuman is approached not for his own sake but as the surest gateway to Rama-bhakti. Many devotees recite this doha three times at the end of each path. (Source: HSNEF Hanuman Chalisa English Meaning PDF)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Hanuman Chalisa connected to the Ramcharitmanas?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, deeply. Tulsidas composed both works, and the Hanuman Chalisa functions as a devotional companion to the larger Ramcharitmanas, his Awadhi retelling of the Ramayana. Several chaupais of the Chalisa directly summarise episodes that appear at length in the Sundara Kanda and Lanka Kanda of the Ramcharitmanas — Hanuman crossing the ocean, finding Sita in the Ashoka grove, burning Lanka, and reviving Lakshmana with the Sanjivani herb. Many traditional households read the Sundara Kanda on Tuesdays followed by the Hanuman Chalisa, treating the two as a single devotional unit. The Chalisa's emphasis on Hanuman as the perfect Rama-bhakta is a distillation of the broader Ramcharitmanas worldview. (Sources: Wikipedia; Radhakrishna Temple)
